Zhang Yang, styledZhishu, was fromYunzhong[雲中].
Because he supportedBing province[并州] through his valor and fierceness, he was madeClerk of Martial Fierceness.
During the end of the reign of theKeenEmperor, the realm under heaven was in turmoil.Jian Shuo, who was a loved and favoredJunior Gentlemen of the Yellow Gatesof theEmperor, was appointedColonel of the Upper Armyof the Western Garden Army, the army being at the capital, so as to control the four directions. The powerful and mighty of the world were summoned to serve as his deputies and lieutenants. TheGrand ProgenitorandYuan Shaowere all madeColonelssubordinate to him.ATheInspectorofBing province[并州刺史],Ding Yuan, sent[Zhang] Yangto lead troops to[Jian] Shuoas temporaryMajor.
TheAnnals of the Keen Emperorstates: TheHousehold General of the Rapid Tigers,Yuan Shao, was madeColonel of the Central Army; theColonel of the Garrison Cavalry,Bao Hong, was madeColonel of the Lower Army;Gentleman ConsultantCao Caowas madeCommandant for Military Standards;Zhao RongandFeng Fangwere madeColonels who Assists the Army; andXia MouandChunyu Qiongwere madeColonel of the Left ArmyandColonel of the Right Army.
When theKeenEmperordied,[Jian] Shuowas killed byHe Jin.[Zhang] Yangwas sent by[He] Jinback to his home province to recruit troops. He received over one thousand people and hence stationed atShangdang[上黨], attacking the mountain bandits. When[He] Jinfell,Dong Zhuowrecked chaos.[Zhang] Yangthus was to attack theGrand AdministratorofShangdang[上黨太守] atHukou pass[壺關], but it did not fall, so he raided the counties. His army numbered in the thousands.
When the troops of theShandong Region[山東] revolted, they intended to executeDong Zhuo. WhenYuan ShaoreachedHenei[河內],[Zhang] Yangand[Yuan] Shaoallied together, and they also did the same with theChanyuof the Xiongnu,Yufuluo, and they garrisoned atZhang River[漳水]. TheChanyuwanted to rebel, but[Yuan] Shaoand[Zhang] Yangdid not follow him. TheChanyuseized[Zhang] Yangand they both left, but[Yuan] Shaosent his general,Qu Yi, to chase and strike them south ofYe[鄴], and defeated them. TheChanyuseized[Zhang] Yangand reachedLiyang[黎陽]. They attacked and defeated the army of theGeneral who Crosses the Liao,Geng Zhi, and so their forces were motivated again.[Dong] Zhuoappointed[Zhang] YangasGeneral who Establishes RighteousnessandGrand AdministratorofHenei[河內太守].
When theSon of Heavenwas inHedong[河東],[Zhang] Yangled troops toAnyi[安邑] and was conferred to beGeneral who Stabilizes the Nationand enfeoffed asMarquisofJinyang[晉陽侯].[Zhang] Yangwanted to welcome theSon of Heavenback toLuo[洛], but his generals did not listen, so[Zhang] Yangreturned toYewang[野王]. In the first year of theera [196],Yang Feng,Dong Cheng, andHan Xianforced theSon of Heavento return to the former capital. Their grain was depleted, but[Zhang] Yangreceived them with grain on the roads, and they arrived atLuoyang[洛陽]. He told his generals, “TheSon of Heavenshould be together with the realm under heaven. He is blessed to have the high-rankingExcellenciesandMinisters. I shall guard against external threats; what business have I in the capital?” He thus returned toYewang[野王] and was conferred to serve asGrand Marshal.B
TheRecords of Famed Heroesstates:[Zhang] Yangwas benevolent and harmonious by nature, being without harsh punishments. Whenever his subjects plotted to rebel, then upon them being exposed, they would weep tears in answering. He would always forgive them and make no interrogations.
[Zhang] YangandLü Buwere always friendly with each other, and when theGrand Progenitorsieged[Lü] Bu,[Zhang] Yangwanted to rescue[Lü] Bu, but was not able to. He thus marched his army to the eastern market,1making a show of force from afar. His general,Yang Chou, killed[Zhang] Yangso as to respond with theGrand Progenitor, but a general of[Zhang] Yang,Sui Gu, killed[Yang] Chouand led the troops. He wanted to go north to joinYuan Shao, so theGrand ProgenitorsentShi Huanto intercept and strike him, defeating him atQuancheng[犬城].[Sui] Guwas beheaded and all of the troops wereabsorbed.C•••
This was likely the eastern market inYewang. Notably, the eastern market was also a place where criminals were executed.↩
TheSummarized Documentsstates:[Sui] Guwas styled Baitu [白兔]. He killedYang Chouand garrisoned his army atShequan[射犬]. At the time, a sorcerer warned[Sui] Gu, saying, “General, your courtesy name has “tu” [兔; hare] in it and the name of the city has “quan” [犬; dog] in it. When a hare encounters a dog, it will be startled due to the balance of power and will urgently seek to flee.”[Sui] Gudid not listen, and thus died in battle.

Zhang Yang (Zhishu)

AbstractEntity::updateTimestamp: Timestamp 'created_at' changed to '1745032417' from 'NULL' via 'AbstractEntity::loadEntityCache::data[db][*]'
Zhang Yang, styled Zhishu, came from Yunzhong. Because of his reknown for courage in Bingzhou, he was made Martially Fierce Assistant Officer. Towards the end of Emperor Ling’s reign, all under heaven was in chaos. The Emperor pandered to the desires of Junior Attendants at the Yellow Gates Jian Shuo, making him General-in-Chief of the Grand Army of the Western Garden and Commandant of the Capital Troops, intending to use him as the conduit to pass on imperial orders to all. All the heroes under heaven viewed this as a biased decision.
The Great Ancestor,1Yuan Shao and others were made Colonels.A•••
Cao Cao.↩
According toThe Records of Emperor Ling: The General of the Gentleman of the Households who Exhibits the Ferocity of a Tiger Yuan Shao became Colonel of the Centre Army, Colonel of the Garrison Cavalry Bao Hong became Colonel of the Lower Army, Cao Cao became Colonel that sets Examples to the Army, Zhao Rong and Feng Fang became Colonels who Assist the Army, Xia Mou and Chunyu Qiong became the Colonels of the Left and Right.
Bingzhou Inspector Ding Yuan appointed Zhang Yang as Acting Major and sent him to visit Jian Shuo. After Emperor Ling’s death, Jian Shuo was killed by He Jin. Under He Jin’s orders, Zhang Yang returned to his own province to recruit soldiers. He raised in excess of a thousand men and stationed himself at Shangdang, from where he attacked mountain bandits. After He Jin’s demise, Dong Zhuo spread disorder. Zhang Yang led his troops to attack the Governor of Shangdang at Huguan. While he was unsuccessful there, he captured a few counties and the size of his contingent rose to several thousand. Later, armies were raised in Shandong with the intention of punishing Dong Zhuo.
When Yuan Shao was at Henei, Zhang Yang joined him and Yu Fuluo, the Xiongnu Chanyu,2to station troops at Zhangshui. When the Chanyu revolted, neither Yuan Shao nor Zhang Yang followed his lead. The Chanyu forced Zhang Yang into joining him, and Yuan Shao sent his general Qu Yi to attack them just south of Ye, where Yu Fuluo was defeated. Yu Fuluo took Zhang Yang to Liyang, there defeating the army of Geng Zhi, General who Crosses the Liao, and their forcerecovered.•••
Chanyu is title of Xiongnu rulers.↩
Dong Zhuo appointed Zhang Yang to the posts of General who Establishes Righteousness and Governor of Henei. When the Son of Heaven was at Hedong, Zhang Yang led his troops to Anyi, receiving an appointment as General who Brings Peace to the Nation and was enfeoffed as Marquis of Jinyang. Zhang Yang wanted to bring the Son of Heaven back to Luoyang, but the other generals rejected his proposal, so he returned to Yewang. In the first year of Establishing Calm (196 AD), Yang Feng, Dong Cheng and Han Xian were taking the Son of Heaven to return to the old capital and met with a shortage of provisions. Zhang Yang sent provisions to supply them along the way and escorted them to Luoyang. To all the generals, he said “The fate of all under heaven is bound up with with that of the Son of Heaven, and it is fortunate that he has such honourable ministers and great officials. As Yang hails from distant lands, what business have I in the capital?” He forthwith returned to Yewang. He was appointed Commander-in-Chief.B
According to theRecord of Heroes: Zhang Yang was by nature harmonious and was lacking when it came to imposing his authority. When his subordinates were discovered to have plotted rebellion, they faced him with tears and he would accept this blithely without enquiring into the roots of the problem.
Zhang Yang had a warm relationship with Lü Bu. When Lü Bu was surrounded by the Great Ancestor, Zhang Yang wished to rescue him but lacked the ability to do so. He deployed his troops out at Dongshi to act as a diversionary force. His general Yang Chou killed him and attempted to surrender to the Great Ancestor. Another general of Zhang Yang’s, Sui Gu, then killed Yang Chou and took the whole host north to join Yuan Shao. Cao Cao sent Shi Huan to attack him, defeating him at Quancheng. Sui Gu was executed and Shi Huan accepted the entire defeated force into his own.
According to Dian Lüe: Sui Gu was styled Baitu. After killing Yang Chou, he encmaped at Shequan. At that time, a sorcerer told Sui Gu “General, your style is Rabbit while the city’s name is Dog[3]. When a rabbit sees a dog, it is naturally afraid. You ought to move elsewhere as soon as possible.” Sui Gu did not heed this advice and was killed as a result of thebattle.3•••
Sui Gu’s style Baitu means White Rabbit, Quancheng means Dog City and Shequan means Dog Shooting.↩
